new_value: all steps complete
Implemented tree-sitter-chacana grammar with 94 tests, external scanner, highlight/validation queries, TOML injection queries, and CI job. Commit c5316dc.

{"description":"VS Code does not support tree-sitter natively for custom languages. Create a TextMate grammar (.tmLanguage.json) derived from the tree-sitter grammar.js to provide syntax highlighting in VS Code and other TextMate-compatible editors (Sublime Text, Atom, GitHub linguist). Scope: source.chacana. File extension: .chcn. Should cover all constructs: tensors, indices, variance markers, functional operators, symmetrization/anti-symmetrization, derivatives, perturbation, commutator, scalars, and Greek identifiers. Reference: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js for the canonical grammar rules, tree-sitter-chacana/queries/highlights.scm for highlight groupings."}

Reference: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js for the canonical grammar rules, tree-sitter-chacana/queries/highlights.scm for highlight groupings.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T21:00:02Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T21:00:02Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Build a Language Server Protocol server that provides real-time diagnostics for Chacana expressions (.chcn files). The LSP combines tree-sitter incremental parsing (tree-sitter-chacana WASM/C) with the static type checker to deliver: (1) syntax error highlighting as-you-type via tree-sitter error recovery nodes, (2) index variance mismatch diagnostics via validation.scm queries, (3) rank/pattern errors by cross-referencing the TOML GlobalContext, (4) go-to-definition for tensor names (jump to TOML declaration), (5) hover info showing tensor rank, index pattern, and symmetries. Architecture: tree-sitter for incremental parse → S-expression queries for fast syntactic diagnostics → full checker pass on save for semantic diagnostics. File extension: .chcn. Should work with any LSP-compatible editor (VS Code, Neovim, Helix, Emacs, Zed). Depends on: tree-sitter-chacana grammar (done), Python checker (done), TOML context loader (done)."}

new_value: {"notes":"Decision: TypeScript implementation. Uses web-tree-sitter with existing WASM binary. Ships as VS Code extension with bundled LSP. Checker rules (~365 LOC Python) to be ported to TS. TextMate grammar (chacana-lfx) folded into same extension package."}

new_value: TextMate grammar shipped as part of vscode-chacana extension (syntaxes/chacana.tmLanguage.json). Commit 8955c09.

Complete LSP server implemented in TypeScript. Syntax diagnostics, type checking (5 rules), hover (tensor info + operator docs), go-to-definition (tensor -> TOML), context resolution (directive + sibling walk), TOML file watching. 77 passing tests. Browser checker bundle powers the static playground. Commit 38ba2e2.
{"priority":4}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-8g5","title":"README links to contrib/ and queries/ without tree-sitter-chacana/ prefix","description":"README.md:75-76 references queries/highlights.scm, queries/validation.scm, and contrib/toml-injections.scm without the tree-sitter-chacana/ directory prefix. These paths don't resolve from the repo root. Users clicking these links from GitHub would get 404s.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:53Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:53Z"}

new_value: {"priority":3}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-9jr","title":"Root .gitignore missing node_modules/ entry","description":"The root .gitignore does not include node_modules/. The directory exists untracked. Any git add -A or git add . would accidentally stage it. The vscode-chacana/.gitignore has it but root does not.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:48Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:48Z"}

new_value: {"title":"feat: add unary negation support — -T{^a _b} is currently a parse error"}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-ejd","title":"No unary negation support — -T{^a _b} is a parse error","description":"The grammar rule sum_expr is product_expr, ZeroOrMore([\"+\", \"-\"], product_expr), requiring a product_expr first. Leading - for unary negation is not supported, which is a significant expressiveness gap for a mathematical DSL.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:06Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:06Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"FIX DIRECTION: Restrict functional_op to match only known operator keywords (d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, hodge, i) using an inline choice instead of generic identifier. Do NOT use word property as it affects the whole grammar."}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-ghu","title":"Any identifier(...) parsed as functional_op — no implicit tensor multiplication with parenthesized expression","description":"Because functional_op appears before tensor_expr in _primary and matches identifier '(' ... ')', R(A+B) is always parsed as a function call, never as tensor R times parenthesized (A+B). Only known operator names (d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, hodge, i) should match as functional_op. Consider restricting via word property or keyword list.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:21Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:21Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"FIX DIRECTION: Use threading.Lock around the check-then-write pattern in _get_parser(). Parser creation is expensive so per-call creation is not ideal. threading.local() is another option if concurrent parsing is needed."}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-twv","title":"Global _cached_parser creates race conditions in multi-threaded use","description":"_get_parser() (grammar.py:151-159) uses a bare global _cached_parser with check-then-write pattern. In multi-threaded environments, two threads can both create parsers, and sharing a single Arpeggio parser instance across concurrent parse() calls corrupts internal parser state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:38:57Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:38:57Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"SCOPE: This is an architectural redesign, not a simple bug fix. Requires a design phase before implementation. Consider splitting into sub-tasks: (1) multi-expression grammar support, (2) document-level parsing strategy, (3) tree storage rewrite, (4) position mapping update, (5) hover/definition update."}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-icb","title":"Line-by-line parsing makes multi-line expressions impossible","description":"server.ts:78-120 splits the document by newlines and parses each line independently. Multi-line expressions are fundamentally impossible. All AST SourceRange positions have startLine:0/endLine:0 from single-line parsing, requiring manual line number override. This architecture severely limits the language server.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:03Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:03Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — section on nested symmetrization rejection"}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-pif","title":"Nested symmetrization accepted by grammar, violating spec requirement to reject","description":"The _index_element rule in grammar.js allows symmetrization/anti_symmetrization as choices, and the index_list inside symmetrization uses the same _index_element rule. This means T{_( _( a b _) _)} successfully parses. The PEG spec explicitly states 'the parser MUST reject recursive symmetrization.' Need a separate _sym_index_element that only allows $.index.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:19Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:19Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — Unicode scope requirements"}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-pep","title":"Identifier regex accepts entire U+0370-03FF block including unassigned/Coptic characters","description":"The identifier regex [a-zA-Z\\u0370-\\u03FF] (grammar.js:167) includes unassigned code points, reserved characters, and Coptic letters that aren't Greek. U+037E (Greek question mark, looks like semicolon) could cause visual spoofing. Should be tightened to actual Greek letters used in physics (e.g., \\u0391-\\u03C9).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:23Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:23Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — variance marker matching requirement"}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-h8k","title":"Mismatched variance markers in symmetrization silently accepted","description":"Grammar allows T{_( a b ^)} — covariant opening with contravariant closing. This parses with no error. Mathematical convention and the spec require opening/closing variance markers to match. Should be enforced in the grammar or external scanner.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:22Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:22Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — scalar production rule"}
old_value: {"id":"chacana-2hs","title":"Integer/float split in scalar rule diverges from PEG spec","description":"The scalar rule uses choice($.float, $.integer) with separate regexes. PEG spec defines scalar as a single [0-9]+ ('.' [0-9]+)? production. The split means 3. (trailing dot) parses as integer+ERROR instead of a clear scalar error. Consider a single /[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?/ regex.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"saxa.kile@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:26Z","created_by":"sashakile","updated_at":"2026-03-19T22:39:26Z"}
